questions.q: Which party dominated the Indian politics from 1947 to 1967?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was the dominant party in Indian politics from 1947 to 1967. It was headed by Jawaharlal Nehru and later by Lal Bahadur Shastri. The party played a crucial role in shaping India's policy and governance during this period.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister of India, serving from 1947 to 1964. He played a crucial role in shaping India's foreign policy and was a key figure in the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: What was the significance of the 1967 Assembly elections?
opts: (A) The Congress lost power in most states
(B) The Communist Party of India gained power in most states
(C) The Bharatiya Jan Sangh became the dominant party
(D) The Socialist Party emerged as a major force
questions.explanation: The 1967 Assembly elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The Congress lost power in most states, marking the end of its dominance. This led to the emergence of non-Congress governments in several states.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Bharatiya Jan Sangh?
questions.explanation: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya was the leader of the Bharatiya Jan Sangh, a right-wing political party in India. He played a crucial role in shaping the party's ideology and policies.

questions.q: What was the impact of the Emergency on Indian politics?
opts: (A) The Congress became even more dominant
(C) The Emergency led to a decline in democratic institutions
(D) The Emergency resulted in a brief period of stability
questions.explanation: The Emergency imposed by the Congress government in 1975-77 led to a decline in democratic institutions. The government suspended civil liberties, and the opposition parties were suppressed.
questions.topic: Impact of Emergency on Indian Politics

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister during the Emergency?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister of India during the Emergency from 1975 to 1977. She imposed the Emergency and suspended civil liberties, leading to widespread opposition.

questions.q: What was the significance of the 1977 General Elections?
opts: (A) The Congress lost power at the Centre
(B) The opposition parties gained power in most states
(C) The Bharatiya Jan Sangh became the dominant party
(D) The Socialist Party emerged as a major force
questions.explanation: The 1977 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The Congress lost power at the Centre, and the Janata Party, an opposition coalition, came to power.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Janata Party?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was the leader of the Janata Party, an opposition coalition that came to power in 1977. He was the Prime Minister of India from 1977 to 1979.

questions.q: What was the impact of the 1980 General Elections on Indian politics?
opts: (A) The Congress regained power at the Centre
(C) The Emergency led to a brief period of stability
(D) The Janata Party became the dominant party
questions.explanation: The 1980 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The Congress regained power at the Centre, and Indira Gandhi became the Prime Minister again.
questions.topic: Impact of Elections on Indian Politics

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister during the 1980-84 period?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister of India from 1980 to 1984. She played a crucial role in shaping India's policy and governance during this period.

questions.q: What was the significance of the 1984 General Elections?
opts: (A) The Congress lost power at the Centre
(B) The opposition parties gained power in most states
(C) The Bharatiya Jan Sangh became the dominant party
(D) The death of Indira Gandhi led to a brief period of instability
questions.explanation: The 1984 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The Congress lost power at the Centre for the first time since 1967, and Rajiv Gandhi became the Prime Minister.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) during the 1980s?
questions.explanation: Atal Bihari Vajpayee was the leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) during the 1980s. He played a crucial role in shaping the party's ideology and policies.

questions.q: What was the impact of the 1989 General Elections on Indian politics?
opts: (A) The Congress regained power at the Centre
(C) The Emergency led to a brief period of stability
(D) The Janata Party became the dominant party
questions.explanation: The 1989 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The opposition parties gained strength, and the Congress lost power at the Centre.
questions.topic: Impact of Elections on Indian Politics

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister during the 1989-91 period?
questions.explanation: V.P. Singh was the Prime Minister of India from 1989 to 1990. He played a crucial role in shaping India's policy and governance during this period.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Mandal Commission?
opts: (A) It led to a significant increase in reservation for SCs and STs
(B) It recommended a uniform civil code
(C) It led to a brief period of instability in the country
(D) It recommended a reduction in reservation for SCs and STs
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission, set up in 1979, recommended a significant increase in reservation for SCs and STs. The report was implemented in 1990, leading to widespread protests and instability.
questions.topic: Social and Economic Policies of the One-Party Dominance

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) during the 1990s?
questions.explanation: Lal Krishna Advani was the leader of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) during the 1990s. He played a crucial role in shaping the party's ideology and policies.

questions.q: What was the impact of the 1991 General Elections on Indian politics?
opts: (A) The Congress regained power at the Centre
(C) The Emergency led to a brief period of stability
(D) The Janata Party became the dominant party
questions.explanation: The 1991 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The opposition parties gained strength, and the Congress lost power at the Centre.
questions.topic: Impact of Elections on Indian Politics

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister during the 1991-96 period?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao was the Prime Minister of India from 1991 to 1996. He played a crucial role in shaping India's policy and governance during this period.

questions.q: What was the significance of the 1996 General Elections?
opts: (A) The Congress regained power at the Centre
(C) The Emergency led to a brief period of stability
(D) The Janata Party became the dominant party
questions.explanation: The 1996 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The opposition parties gained strength, and the Congress lost power at the Centre.
questions.topic: Impact of Elections on Indian Politics

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister during the 1996-98 period?
questions.explanation: H.D. Deve Gowda was the Prime Minister of India from 1996 to 1997. He played a crucial role in shaping India's policy and governance during this period.

questions.q: What was the significance of the 1998 General Elections?
opts: (A) The Congress regained power at the Centre
(C) The Emergency led to a brief period of stability
(D) The BJP became the dominant party
questions.explanation: The 1998 General Elections marked a significant shift in Indian politics. The BJP, led by Atal Bihari Vajpayee, became the dominant party and formed the government at the Centre.
